question.text: What is the minimum application size per application in an SME issue?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The minimum application size in an SME issue is Rs. 1 lakh per application. This higher threshold compared to Main Board IPOs reflects the higher risk profile of SME companies and ensures only investors with adequate risk appetite participate. **The Trap:** Rs. 10,000-15,000 is the typical lot size for Main Board IPOs. Rs. 2 lakh is the minimum investment for AIFs in certain categories. Rs. 1 lakh specifically applies to SME issues. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX tests minimum application sizes across different issue types — SME (Rs. 1 lakh), Main Board (varies), and institutional placements (higher). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Rs. 1 lakh minimum acts as a natural filter — ensure your investors understand SME risks before they commit this amount.
